blockingIssues.description: The assigned feature required a concrete fix for the renewed VAL-CROSS-002 divergence, but the reviewed commit does not modify this test or any timer-state convergence code. The handoff says no code changes were needed and attributes the failure to discarded uncommitted files, which means there is no committed implementation proving the fix requested by the feature description.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reinforce in `frontend-test-worker` reviews and orchestration that fix-feature handoffs must cite a commit that actually contains the scoped implementation, or explicitly state the behavior was already fixed earlier and return the feature instead of claiming success.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Handoff 2026-03-25T09-33-37-198Z__tracking-shell-direct-entry-convergence-regression-fix-2__22364c96-83ae-4c52-beb2-20491deb0df6.json reports commit 656d581 and 'No code changes were needed'. `.factory/skills/frontend-test-worker/SKILL.md` says to verify the commitId directly contains the feature's code changes and not to treat rerun-only passes as completion for flaky-fix features. `git show 656d581 --stat` only includes apps/website/src/features/tracking/overview-views.tsx.

override.reason: Current validator evidence is green: `vp run test -r` passes, including `VAL-CROSS-002`, and `vp run check -r` passes. The remaining scrutiny failure is traceability-only: the follow-up feature resolved stray uncommitted drift and restored the already-correct committed behavior, so there is no live product or validator gap left to fix in `tracking-ui-followup-2`.
